texto: But at the same time, there must never be the least hesitation in giving up a position the moment it is shown to be untenable. It is not going too far to say that the greatness of a scientific investigator does not rest on the fact of his having never made a mistake, but rather on his readiness to admit that he has done so, whenever the contrary evidence is cogent enough.
explicacao: Orientação direta de William Bayliss sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “But at the same time, there must never be the least hesitation in giving up a position the moment…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a tempo, evidence, greatness, útil em momentos de greatness e hesitation.
